claimText: The Supreme Court has banned tiger safaris and night tourism in the core areas and designated tiger corridors of tiger reserves.
evidenceExcerpt: SC banned tiger safaris and night tourism in core areas/designated tiger corridors.
examUse: Statement-based question on permitted activities in core vs. buffer zones of tiger reserves.
distractorHooks: Safaris are allowed in core areas under strict regulation
Night tourism is permitted in core areas for research purposes

claimText: Tiger safaris are permitted only on non-forest or degraded forest land within the buffer zones of tiger reserves.
evidenceExcerpt: Safaris are permitted only on non-forest or degraded forest land in buffer zones.
examUse: Statement-based question on conditions for safaris in tiger reserves.
distractorHooks: Safaris are permitted on pristine forest land in buffer zones
Safaris are allowed anywhere in the buffer zone

claimText: Commercial mining and major hydroelectric projects are prohibited in the buffer areas of tiger reserves.
evidenceExcerpt: Commercial mining and major hydro projects are prohibited in buffer areas.
examUse: Statement-based question on restricted activities in buffer zones.
distractorHooks: Commercial mining is allowed in buffer zones with NTCA approval
Major hydro projects are permitted in buffer zones

claimText: Human-Wildlife Conflicts (HWCs) are to be treated as Natural Disasters, providing a uniform 10 lakh ex-gratia for human deaths.
evidenceExcerpt: Human-Wildlife Conflicts (HWCs) to be granted Natural Disaster status with 10 lakh ex-gratia for human deaths.
examUse: Statement-based question on the classification and compensation for HWCs.
distractorHooks: HWCs are treated as man-made disasters
Compensation varies by state without a uniform mandate

claimText: Tiger Reserves are notified by State Governments under the Wildlife Protection Act (WPA) 1972 on the advice of the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA).
evidenceExcerpt: Reserves are notified by State Governments under WPA 1972 on the advice of NTCA.
examUse: Statement-based question on the authority responsible for declaring tiger reserves.
distractorHooks: Declared under the Environment Protection Act 1986

claimText: Altering the boundaries of a Tiger Reserve requires the recommendation of the NTCA and the approval of the National Board for Wild Life (NBWL).
evidenceExcerpt: Boundary alterations of Tiger Reserves require NTCA recommendation and NBWL approval.
examUse: Statement-based question on the statutory requirements for altering tiger reserve boundaries.
